_protocol: Calibration backfill — resolved topic for Brier score validation

meta.title: 750 GeV Diphoton Excess
meta.question: Is the ~750 GeV diphoton excess observed by ATLAS and CMS at the LHC a new particle or a statistical fluctuation?
meta.resolution: Resolved when full Run 2 data (2016) confirms or excludes the excess at 5-sigma significance. If excess disappears with more data, H2 wins.

directionHints.DATA: depends on direction — excess grows or shrinks
directionHints.THEORETICAL: mostly H1/H3 — theory papers either explain or debunk

H1.label: New particle — genuine resonance at ~750 GeV

H2.label: Statistical fluctuation — disappears with more data

H4.label: BSM physics but not a simple resonance (e.g., continuum, interference)

posteriorHistory.note: Initial priors after ATLAS/CMS December presentations. ~3.6 sigma local (ATLAS), ~2.6 sigma local (CMS). Skeptical — look-elsewhere effect not fully accounted for, and 2-sigma excesses appear and vanish regularly.
Indicator anti_h3_consistent_mass fired (firing #1, decay=1.000): REPLAY: Fired at initial presentation. Both experiments saw excess at ~750 GeV. This arg

redTeam.challenge: No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.

posteriorHistory.note: Indicator anti_h1_no_corroboration fired (firing #1, decay=1.000): REPLAY: Fired. No ZZ, Zh, WW, or fermionic channels showed anything at 750 GeV.

redTeam.challenge: No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.

posteriorHistory.note: Indicator t1_excess_disappears fired (firing #1, decay=1.000): REPLAY: Fired at ICHEP 2016. Both experiments showed the bump gone.

redTeam.challenge: No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.

posteriorHistory.note: RE-LOCK POST-REPLAY: winner=H2. ATLAS+CMS 2016 Moriond: excess vanished with full Run 2. Statistical fluctuation. (Re-locked after erroneous replay.)

tier1_critical.desc: Either ATLAS or CMS reports >=5 sigma local significance for diphoton excess at ~750 GeV with full 2016 dataset
Full 2016 data shows the excess is <2 sigma local in BOTH experiments
tier1_critical.note: REPLAY: Fired at ICHEP 2016. Both experiments showed the bump gone.

tier2_strong.desc: Excess seen in a second decay channel (ZZ, Zh, WW) at ~750 GeV
tier2_strong.note: Never fired. Absence of corroboration was itself informative.

tier2_strong.desc: Updated analysis shows narrow width consistent with single resonance
tier2_strong.note: ATLAS actually showed wider width at Moriond, making resonance interpretation harder.

tier2_strong.desc: ATLAS and CMS report the excess at significantly different masses (>10 GeV apart) or one experiment sees it while the other does not at >2 sigma. Observable: comparison of fitted parameters in conference presentations or publications.
tier2_strong.note: Never fired — both experiments reported consistent mass. Had this fired, it would strongly suggest detector artifact or analysis error rather than real physics. A genuine particle would appear at the same mass in both detectors.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Theoretical community converges on a single compelling BSM explanation
tier3_suggestive.note: 500+ papers proposed 500+ different models. No convergence — noise, not signal.

anti_indicators.desc: No corroborating excess in any other channel after 6+ months
anti_indicators.note: REPLAY: Fired. No ZZ, Zh, WW, or fermionic channels showed anything at 750 GeV.

anti_indicators.desc: Both ATLAS and CMS report the excess at consistent mass and width within statistical uncertainties. Observable: comparison of fitted mass peaks and width parameters in published results.
anti_indicators.note: REPLAY: Fired at initial presentation. Both experiments saw excess at ~750 GeV. This arg

actorModel.description: Physics experiment dynamics — no geopolitical actors, but institutional incentives matter

atlas.name: ATLAS Collaboration
atlas.role: One of two general-purpose LHC detectors
biases: Publication bias — incentive to present interesting results
overrides: Conference pressure can lead to preliminary results before full review

cms.name: CMS Collaboration
cms.role: Second general-purpose LHC detector — independent cross-check
biases: Competitive pressure to confirm/deny ATLAS results quickly

theory_community.name: Theoretical Physics Community
theory_community.role: Generates explanations, not evidence. Theory papers are RHETORIC, not DATA.
biases: Strong incentive to publish quickly on hot topics
Confirmation bias — easier to get a model published if it explains the excess
overrides: Social media hype can drive theory production faster than data warrants

methodology: DATA OVER THEORY — 500 theory papers are not 500 data points
LOOK-ELSEWHERE EFFECT — local significance is not global significance
INDEPENDENT REPLICATION — CMS seeing it too matters, but only if analysis pipelines are truly independent
ABSENCE OF EVIDENCE IS EVIDENCE — no corroborating channels after 6 months is informative

evidenceLog.text: ATLAS reports ~3.6 sigma local excess in diphoton channel at ~750 GeV using 3.2 fb-1 of 13 TeV data. CMS reports ~2.6 sigma local at similar mass. Both presented at CERN end-of-year seminar.
Over 500 theoretical papers posted to arXiv proposing explanations for the 750 GeV excess. Models range from heavy Higgs to sgoldstinos to composite states. No convergence on a single explanation.
evidenceLog.note: Theory proliferation is noise. 500 models explaining one bump means the bump is underdetermined, not confirmed.

evidenceLog.text: Moriond 2016 update: ATLAS shows wider resonance width (~45 GeV) than December. CMS significance slightly reduced with refined analysis. Global significance for combination remains below 3 sigma.
No corroborating excess found in ZZ, Zh, WW, or fermionic channels at ~750 GeV after 6 months of searches. If this were a real heavy scalar, some branching ratio to other channels would be expected.
evidenceLog.note: Absence of corroboration is evidence against H1. A real particle should show up somewhere else.

evidenceLog.text: ICHEP 2016: both ATLAS and CMS present full 2016 dataset. The 750 GeV diphoton excess has vanished completely. ATLAS sees no significant excess with 15.4 fb-1. CMS confirms with 12.9 fb-1. The bump was a statistical fluctuation.
LR MIGRATION RESET: posteriors reset to design priors; evidence log preserved.

TOPIC RESOLUTION LOCK: posteriors set to H2=1.0 (winning hypothesis). Rationale: ATLAS+CMS 2016 Moriond results: excess vanished with full Run 2 dataset (~16/fb). Statistical fluctuation confirmed. Both collaborations reported no significant excess remained.
REPLAY BEGIN: 3 previously-fired indicators will be re-applied chronologically under current LR system. Posteriors reset to design priors; indicator state cleared. Evidence log preserved for audit.

REPLAY: indicator t1_excess_disappears fires at historical firedDate 2016-08-05. Original note: Fired at ICHEP 2016. Both experiments showed the bump gone.

warnings: Hypotheses {'H4'} have no anti-indicator — what evidence would make these LESS likely?
